name: Allies
description:
Mortals who support and assist you in a specific area of influence. They act willingly but have their own lives and limits.
1 — A single useful contact who will do small favors.
2 — A few reliable allies in one sphere (police, media, etc.).
3 — Several well-placed allies who will take moderate risks.
4 — A network of influential allies across an organization.
5 — Powerful allies at the highest levels who will take serious risks for you.

name: Contacts
description:
People who provide information across various social spheres. They share intel but won't take action or personal risks for you.
1 — One sphere of contacts (street, academic, corporate).
2 — Two spheres of contacts.
3 — Three spheres, with some well-placed sources.
4 — Four spheres, including hard-to-access circles.
5 — Five or more spheres; you can find information on almost anything given time.

name: Domain
description:
Territory you control or have been granted by Kindred authority. Your domain provides feeding grounds and political standing.
1 — A single block or small building (a bar, a shop).
2 — A few blocks or a small neighborhood.
3 — A significant neighborhood or district.
4 — A large district or multiple neighborhoods.
5 — A major section of the city; you are a power in the domain game.

name: Fame
description:
Mortal notoriety and public recognition. Adds dice to Social rolls with mortals who recognize you but makes anonymity harder.
1 — Locally known (neighborhood celebrity, local band, popular blogger).
2 — Regionally known (city-wide reputation, minor media presence).
3 — Nationally known (celebrity status, social media influencer, published author).

name: Haven
description:
The security and quality of your daytime resting place. A good Haven protects against intrusion, sunlight, and discovery.
1 — A basic crash pad: an abandoned building, a rented storage unit, minimal security.
2 — A decent apartment or house with locks and covered windows. Reasonably safe.
3 — A well-secured location: reinforced doors, alarm system, no windows, private access.
4 — A fortified haven: security cameras, multiple locks, hidden entrance, panic room.
5 — A true fortress: multiple redundant security systems, guards or ghouls, concealed underground chambers, virtually impossible to breach.

name: Herd
description:
A group of mortals you feed from regularly — blood dolls, groupies, cultists, or a managed group. Reduces hunting difficulty and Masquerade risk.
1 — 3-5 accessible vessels; you rarely go hungry but options are limited.
2 — 6-10 vessels; comfortable feeding with some variety.
3 — A sizeable herd (10-20); hunting rolls are rarely needed.
4 — A large, well-managed herd (20-30); feeding is almost effortless.
5 — An extensive network (30+); you virtually never need to hunt and can feed others from your herd.

name: Influence
description:
Political or institutional pull in mortal society. Can be used to cover up Masquerade breaches, arrange logistics, block investigations, or pull strings.
1 — Minor influence in a single institution (a precinct, a local paper).
2 — Moderate influence; you can pull strings in one major institution.
3 — Significant influence across one or two institutions.
4 — Major influence; you can shape policy in your area of control.
5 — Vast influence; you are a hidden power behind major mortal institutions.

name: Mask
description:
A carefully maintained mortal identity with documentation, credit history, and social presence. Essential for operating in the modern world and avoiding the Second Inquisition.
1 — A basic fake ID and a burner phone. Won't survive scrutiny.
2 — A functional identity: driver's license, bank account, rental history. Passes casual checks.
3 — A solid identity: employment records, tax history, social media presence. Passes background checks.
4 — A deep cover: years of consistent records, professional reputation, multiple references.
5 — An impeccable identity: impossible to distinguish from a real person's history even under federal investigation.

name: Mawla
description:
A Kindred mentor or patron — an elder or established vampire who offers guidance, protection, or resources. The relationship is not equal; they expect service in return.
1 — An ancilla who occasionally offers advice.
2 — A respected elder who provides guidance and minor favors.
3 — A powerful elder who actively protects and sponsors you.
4 — A very influential Kindred (Primogen, Seneschal) who champions your interests.
5 — One of the most powerful Kindred in the city (Prince, Archbishop) considers you a valued protege.

name: Resources
description:
Your material wealth, income, and financial assets.
1 — A small apartment, a used car, about $1,000/month disposable income.
2 — A nice apartment, a reliable car, about $2,500/month.
3 — A house or condo, a luxury car, about $10,000/month. Comfortable living.
4 — Multiple properties, household staff, about $50,000/month. Upper class.
5 — Vast wealth: mansions, private security, investment portfolios, effectively unlimited personal funds.

name: Retainers
description:
Loyal personal servants — ghouls, employees, or devoted followers. Each dot represents one retainer.
1 — One retainer: a driver, assistant, or lookout.
2 — Two retainers covering different roles.
3 — Three retainers; a small personal staff.
4 — Four retainers; a well-rounded entourage (bodyguard, assistant, hacker, spy).
5 — Five retainers; a professional team capable of complex operations.

name: Status
description:
Your standing and reputation within Kindred society (Camarilla, Anarch Movement, or other sect).
1 — Recognized neonate; you are known and accepted but have no influence.
2 — Respected member; your opinions carry some weight.
3 — Influential ancilla or well-known elder; you have a voice in politics.
4 — Primogen, Baron, or equivalent; you hold formal authority.
5 — Prince, Archbishop, or sect leader; your word is law in your domain.

name: Cobweb
description:
Connection to the Malkavian Madness Network — a psychic web linking all Malkavians. Allows receiving and sending impressions, warnings, and visions through the network. Malkavian only.
1 — Occasional flashes of insight from the network; unreliable and confusing.
2 — Regular reception of impressions; you can sometimes identify their source.
3 — Active participation; you can send and receive specific messages.
4 — Strong connection; you receive early warnings of danger and can broadcast widely.
5 — A nexus in the network; you sense the emotional state of Malkavians city-wide.
